channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: U.S. says it targeted Iranian forces after attacks that killed two American service members
item.description: Centcom says it hit Iran's coastal surveillance and air defense facilities a day after two U.S. troops were killed in an Iranian strike on Jordan.

item.title: American Airlines CEO lays out his vision to close a more than $3 billion profit gap
item.description: The carrier is working on improving reliability, investing in more premium seats and lounges, and considering Boeing and Airbus for a new wide-body plane order.

item.title: Universal and Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey' sails to $124.5 million opening
item.description: Christopher Nolan's "The Odyssey" tallied an estimated $124.5 million at the domestic box office, with IMAX screenings fueling a big haul.

item.title: Housing costs is the top political issue for young voters, CNBC survey finds
item.description: The cost of housing is the top priority for voters ages 18 to 34, besting food and healthcare cost concerns, the CNBC All-America Economic Survey found.

item.title: Earnings playbook: Alphabet and Tesla are among the big companies set to report this week
item.description: The earnings season heats up this week with two megacaps set to release their latest results.

item.title: Cybersecurity risks posed by over-the-air tech in autos has analysts concerned
item.description: The automotive industry's increasing use of over-the-air technology makes it more susceptible to cyberattacks, analysts say.

item.title: The World Cup winner will earn $51 million under expanded purse
item.description: FIFA expanded the payout for the 2026 World Cup, setting a record with a combined $871 million paid to the 48 participating teams.

item.title: The market for psychedelic drugs to treat mental health conditions gets a big buy signal
item.description: Eli Lilly's acquisition of psychedelic drug maker AtaiBeckley extends Big Pharma's embrace of a stigmatized class of medications for treating mental health.

item.title: Kalshi adds 3 million new users as prediction market capitalizes on the World Cup
item.description: The prediction market has launched some ads featuring soccer players, as well as formed a partnership to display its brand in more places during the tournament.

item.title: Top Wall Street analysts suggest these 3 dividend stocks for steady income
item.description: Here are three dividend-paying stocks that are highlighted by Wall Street's top pros, as tracked by TipRanks.

item.title: World Cup audience stayed bipartisan despite Trump's prominent role, CNBC survey finds
item.description: Nearly half of registered voters watched the 2026 World Cup, with income and education shaping viewership more than party affiliation, the survey found.

item.title: Why Dana White thinks much more of UFC's future will be Power Slap and boxing
item.description: UFC president and CEO Dana White predicted that Power Slap may become as big as his flagship fighting sport at the Game Plan conference in New York.

item.title: Russia pounds Kyiv in major ballistic missile attack
item.description: Powerful explosions thundered across the darkened city in an attack that involved 41 missiles of various types and ravaged buildings across several districts.

item.title: Tesla's charts point to a decline ahead, says Carter Worth
item.description: The technical analyst breaks down where he sees Tesla shares going.

item.title: Taylor Farms recalls iceberg lettuce in 27 states due to cyclosporiasis outbreak
item.description: Taylor Farms is recalling iceberg lettuce in 27 states because of the potential that the greens could be contaminated with cyclosporiasis.

item.title: 'WarshGPT': How Wall Street is adapting to the Fed's new era of communication
item.description: Investment firms are readying for an era with less public commentary from the U.S. central bank. Some are relying on AI for an edge.

item.title: AI may make you 'boring' at work, Columbia Business School professor says: How it can hurt your career
item.description: Using AI at work in the wrong ways could lead to "gradually falling behind and becoming complacent," said Sandra Matz, a professor at Columbia Business School.

item.title: Social media influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate arrested in Miami, U.S. Marshals Service tells AP
item.description: The arrests place the U.S. at the center of an international legal saga that has stretched from Romania to Britain.

item.title: Scam victims can owe taxes on stolen money. A bill in Congress could offer relief
item.description: A bill in the House would restore a pre-2018 rule that generally allowed a tax deduction for theft losses and would add other relief for fraud victims.

item.title: Samsung reportedly cuts some U.S. jobs, offers relocations ahead of HQ move
item.description: The Korean electronics giant as reportedly reduced staff at its U.S. display, phone and other consumer electronics operations.

item.title: The AI rotation stole the spotlight from a strong start to earnings season
item.description: The AI trade dominated the market once again this week, upstaging an impressive start to the second-quarter earnings season.

item.title: Chase Sapphire Preferred's 100K bonus is ending soon: 4 reasons to apply now
item.description: Chase Sapphire Preferred's 100K bonus is ending soon: Here's how the 100,000 points could be worth roughly $2,000 in travel.

item.title: Stop asking 'How was work today?' Successful, mentally strong couples ask these 7 questions instead
item.description: This question can get you "stuck in a communication rut," says Amy Morin, author of "13 Things Mentally Strong Couples Don't Do." Here's what to ask instead.

item.title: California's billionaire tax: What's at risk for the biggest state economy in America
item.description: California's billionaire tax would generate quick revenue, but Norway's example shows the longer-term economic impact of targeting the wealthy is unclear.

item.title: Fanatics CEO Michael Rubin on two sports businesses his multibillion-dollar conglomerate won't enter
item.description: Fanatics has become one of the most influential companies in sports, building out a massive merchandise, collectibles and sports gambling platform.

item.title: Two unexpected revelations in Warren Buffett's CNBC interview
item.description: Warren Buffett tells CNBC that Bill Gates' ties to Jeffrey Epstein didn't drive his termination of donations.

item.title: Analysis: Fed Chairman Warsh faces an inflation credibility test after Congress hearings
item.description: The new Fed chairman avoided major stumbles in two days of testimony before the House and Senate, but faces a rapid test of his commitment to price stability.

item.title: Korean beauty products are becoming mainstream in the U.S. Why there may be even more growth ahead
item.description: Morgan Stanley forecasts that K-beauty sales in the U.S. can reach approximately $4 billion in 2026.

item.title: How are prediction markets taxed? The IRS hasn’t provided guidance yet
item.description: Experts say the lack of federal guidance makes it's unclear on how prediction markets winnings should be reported and levied.

item.title: Trump Media pitched $100,000 monthly fee for fastest feed of U.S. president's posts, sources say
item.description: Trump Media & ⁠Technology Group has in recent weeks also pitched a discounted plan of $60,000 per month if the firms sign up for a three-year plan.
